coredata.dc:title: Blue-emitting tryptophan-protected gold nanoclusters acted as a sensitive nanosensor for fluorescence sensing and visual imaging detection of furaltadone
coredata.prism:publicationName: Spectrochimica Acta Part A: Molecular and Biomolecular Spectroscopy
coredata.dc:creator: Cai, Zhifeng
Li, Huinan
Yang, Xin
Zhang, Ming
Guo, Jinhao
Su, Yani
Liu, Taotao
coredata.dc:description: Herein, blue-emitting gold nanoclusters (Au NCs) were carried out through tryptophan as the protecting and reducing agents. In aqueous solution of Au NCs@tryptophan, the addition of furaltadone guaranteed the interaction of furaltadone with tryptophan around Au NCs. The propinquity of furaltadone to Au NCs caused that the fluorescence of Au NCs was weakened by furaltadone based on the inner filter effect (IFE). Under the optimal measurement conditions, the logarithm of relative fluorescence intensity of Au NCs@tryptophan was linearly carried out with the furaltadone amount increasing from 0.5 to 100 μM, the corresponding detection limit was 0.087 μM. The fluorescence change of Au NCs@tryptophan displayed excellent selectivity and sensitivity for furaltadone than other possible substance in the human body. In view of Au NCs@tryptophan, the as-performed fluorescence nanosensor suggested outstanding ability for furaltadone sensing in real samples. Obviously, this nanoprobe of furaltadone could implement the naked-eye visual fluorescence determination of furaltadone.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Gold nanoclusters
Tryptophan
Fluorescence sensing
Furaltadone
Visual imaging

xocs:meta.xocs:srctitle: Spectrochimica Acta Part A: Molecular and Biomolecular Spectroscopy

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Synthesis and characterization of Au NCs@tryptophan
Development and mechanism of Au NCs@tryptophan based fluorescence sensor
Selectivity of Au NCs@tryptophan based fluorescence sensor
Fluorescence determination and visual imaging of furaltadone

xocs:funding.xocs:funding-agency-matched-string: Shanxi Province Science Foundation for Youths
xocs:funding.xocs:funding-agency: Shanxi Province Science Foundation for Youths

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: We would like to thank the Shanxi Province Science Foundation for Youths (No. 20210302124210) and the College Students’ Innovation Program of Taiyuan Normal University (No. CXCY2221) for financial support.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (A) TEM image of Au NCs@tryptophan; (B) Size distribution of Au NCs@tryptophan; (C) XPS spectrum of Au; (D) XPS spectrum of C; (E) XPS spectrum of N; (F) XPS spectrum of O.
(A) The emission spectra under different excitation wavelength; (B) The excitation and emission of Au NCs@tryptophan without and with furaltadone;
(A) The emission spectra of Au NCs@tryptophan with different furaltadone concentration; (B) The linear relationship spectra.
(A) The UV–vis spectrum of furaltadone, excitation and emission spectra of Au NCs@tryptophan; (B) The fluorescence lifetime of Au NCs@tryptophan without and with furaltadone.
The selective graph with different control substances (A, B); The interference graph (C, D).
The graphs of Au NCs@tryptophan with different furaltadone concentrations under sunlight and UV light.
Comparison of different analytical methods for furaltadone determination.
The furaltadone detection in real samples.

head.ce:title: Blue-emitting tryptophan-protected gold nanoclusters acted as a sensitive nanosensor for fluorescence sensing and visual imaging detection of furaltadone

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: College of Chemistry and Materials, Taiyuan Normal University, Jinzhong 030619, PR China
ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: College of Chemistry and Materials, Taiyuan Normal University, Jinzhong 030619, PR China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Herein, blue-emitting gold nanoclusters (Au NCs) were carried out through tryptophan as the protecting and reducing agents. In aqueous solution of Au NCs@tryptophan, the addition of furaltadone guaranteed the interaction of furaltadone with tryptophan around Au NCs. The propinquity of furaltadone to Au NCs caused that the fluorescence of Au NCs was weakened by furaltadone based on the inner filter effect (IFE). Under the optimal measurement conditions, the logarithm of relative fluorescence intensity of Au NCs@tryptophan was linearly carried out with the furaltadone amount increasing from 0.5 to 100 μM, the corresponding detection limit was 0.087 μM. The fluorescence change of Au NCs@tryptophan displayed excellent selectivity and sensitivity for furaltadone than other possible substance in the human body. In view of Au NCs@tryptophan, the as-performed fluorescence nanosensor suggested outstanding ability for furaltadone sensing in real samples. Obviously, this nanoprobe of furaltadone could implement the naked-eye visual fluorescence determination of furaltadone.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: The authors do not have permission to share data.

ce:section.ce:para: Furaltadone is one of nitrofuran antibiotics, which has good antibacterial effect against Escherichia coli and Salmonella, can treat systemic infection, and has advantages of high efficacy, wide Antimicrobial spectrum, etc. It can be used in aquaculture to prevent bacterial infectious diseases and promote growth [1,2]. For a long time, the widespread use of nitrofuran antibiotics has caused drug resistance and residue problems in fishery production. Research has shown that furaltadone and its metabolites in the body have significant toxic side effects, which will ultimately have a significant impact on the health of consumers along the food chain. Therefore, it is necessary to accurately grasp the concentration of furaltadone [3,4]. At present, the study to establish simple, sensitive and efficient measured sensing strategies for furaltadone sensing is of great significance.

Up to now, some measurement strategies have been carried out for furaltadone sensing, such as electrochemical detection method [5–7], liquid chromatography–tandem mass spectrometric (LC–MS/MS) [8], enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) [9], high-performance liquid chromatographic–UV–vis photodiode-array detection (HPLC–DAD) method [10], liquid chromatography–electrospray ionization tandem mass spectrometry [11] and high-performance liquid chromatography with electrochemical detection [12]. These common detection strategies possess the function to detect furaltadone, but the majority of strategies demand high cost equipment, cumbersome operation steps, professional personnel and long sample pre-treatment [13,14]. These unfavorable factors may limit the above strategies for actual applications. On the contrary, the fluorescence detection strategies are facile, efficient, sensitive and low-priced, and can determine the amount of targets to a very low concentration level. Up to present, the fluorescence sensing strategies have not been extensively applied to determine furaltadone. About the fluorescence detection methods, typically the signal source holds fluorescence bioactive molecules or fluorescent nanomaterials. So far, the fluorescent materials mainly include organic dyes, quantum dots and metal nanoclusters. As everyone knows, the fluorescent dyes are generally difficult to degrade and some heavy metals are used to participate in the preparation of quantum dots, which can cause certain harm to the environment. Then, metal nanoclusters have attracted increasing interest due to their unusual characteristics such as strong fluorescence, tiny size and excellent stability [15–17]. As the promising replacer, metal nanoclusters are composed of several to dozens of metal atoms and its average particle size is less than 5 nm, which can be used as the new type of fluorescence probe. As a result of the quantum confinement effect, metal nanoclusters possess excellent biocompatibility and strong fluorescence performance. Specially, the diameter of precious metal nanoclusters is similar to the Fermi wavelength of electrons in the metal conduction band.

Gold nanoclusters (Au NCs) have been used to the researches of chemsensing. As a potential nanomaterial applied for fluorescence nanosensor, Au NCs have possessed an explosion of attention for the sensing of ions, biological small molecules and drug molecules [18,19]. Au NCs-based sensors emerged preferable determination sensitivity, ascribed to the distinctive physicochemical and optical characteristics of Au NCs [20,21]. The small size of Au NCs possesses properties similar to molecules including broad excitation band, large Stokes shift, high quantum yield and nano-size. With the protecting of bioactive small molecules (such as tryptophan), the developed Au NCs possesses excellent biocompatibility and good stability in many complex environments [22,23].

Herein, blue-emitting Au NCs were carried out with the protecting of tryptophan to develop tryptophan-fabricated Au NCs (tryptophan served as both the protective and reducing agent) [24]. The Au NCs@tryptophan were carried out to a new fluorescence nanosensor for the sensing of furaltadone. The fluorescence quenching of furaltadone to Au NCs@tryptophan was ascribed to the inner filter effect (IFE). The fluorescence of Au NCs@tryptophan was attenuated and the fluorescence response changes of Au NCs@tryptophan to furaltadone amount could be used to carry out a new fluorescence system for the sensing of furaltadone. This developed nanosensor could be applied for the detection of furaltadone in real samples and naked-eye visual fluorescence determination of furaltadone.

L-tryptophan (99 %), tetrachloroauric acid (≥99.9 %), furaltadone (98 %), L-glycine (≥99 %), L-alanine (99 %), L-histidine (≥99 %), L-serine (99 %), L-proline (99 %), L-cysteine (99 %), ascorbic acid (≥99 %), glutathione (98 %), urea (99 %), uric acid (99 %), sodium chloride (99.5 %), citric acid (≥99.5 %), sodium citrate dehydrate (99 %), sodium fluoride (≥99 %), sodium bromide (99 %), sodium iodide (99.5 %), sodium carbonate monohydrate (98 %), sodium bicarbonate (≥99.8 %) and sodium sulfate (97 %) were purchased from Aladdin Reagent Co., Ltd. Ultrapure water was obtained from ultrapure water machine.

All fluorescent spectra of Au NCs@tryptophan with and without furaltadone were obtained from F-7000 spectrofluorophotometer (Hitachi, Japan) with the voltage of 400 V and excitation and emission slit of 10 nm/10 nm. The ultraviolet measurements of raw materials and products were developed on the U-4500 UV–Visible spectrophotometer (Hitachi, Japan) with the scanning range of 200–800 nm. The Hitachi H-800 electron microscope at an acceleration voltage of 200 kV was carried out to discuss the morphology and particle size of the Au NCs@tryptophan and the transmission electron microscope (TEM) images were obtained. The Thermo Escalab 250XI spectrometer (Thermo Fish Scientific, America) was utilized to study the oxidation state of elements of the Au NCs@tryptophan.

The synthesis of fluorescent Au NCs@tryptophan was performed according to the reported literature [24]. Typically, 2.5 mM L-tryptophan solution of 10 mL and 1.0 mM tetrachloroauric acid solution of 10 mL were fully mixed under stirring for 5 min. Then, the solution was reacted under 100 ℃ for 4 h. The solution displayed blue fluorescence under UV lamp, indicating the successful preparation of Au NCs@tryptophan. Finally, the Au NCs@tryptophan solution was introduced into the dialysis bag (MW: 1500 Da) for the removal of impurities and was stored in refrigerator.

The measurable determination of furaltadone was performed as follows: 500 μL of Au NCs@tryptophan and 1.5 mL of citric acid-sodium citrate buffer solution (pH = 6) was added into 5 mL of centrifuge tube under stirring for 5 min. Then, different furaltadone concentration solutions were added into the above detection solution to study the linear model based on Au NCs@tryptophan. Finally, the fluorescent data were obtained after incubation for 2 min. The following substances were utilized to discuss the selectivity of the Au NCs@tryptophan: L-glycine, L-alanine, L-histidine, L-serine, L-proline, L-cysteine, ascorbic acid, glutathione, urea, Na+, K+, F−, Cl−, Br−, I−, CO3 2−, HCO3 − and SO4 2−.

To carry out the possibility of Au NCs@tryptophan for furaltadone detection in real samples, lake water samples and glucose samples were obtained from campus and pharmacy. These real samples were processed through centrifugation and filter paper. After that, the standard adjunction strategy was carried out in the presence of different concentration of furaltadone to the lake water samples and glucose samples. Additionally, the following for processes for furaltadone determination were executed as developed above in part 2.3.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Synthesis and characterization of Au NCs@tryptophan
ce:section.ce:para: Herein, amino acid (L-tryptophan) as a chain of protection was applied for the development of Au NCs. Due to its special structure and functional groups, L-tryptophan could be used for both protective agent and reducing agent without another reducers. The complexation between gold atom and functional groups of L-tryptophan suggested via electrostatic binding. In the synthesis, when Au3+ was reduced to Au by L-tryptophan, Au could also chelate with L-tryptophan to form Au NCs. Furthermore, the preparation conditions (such as the molar ratio of Au3+ and L-tryptophan, reaction temperature and reaction time) were discussed. As displayed in Fig. S1, the optimal molar ratio of L-tryptophan and Au3+, reaction temperature and reaction time were 2.5, 100 ℃ and 4 h, respectively.

The as-developed Au NCs@tryptophan were analyzed via different analytical instruments. As displayed in Fig. 1 A, the morphology and particle size of the Au NCs@tryptophan were observed through TEM image, exhibiting that the Au NCs@tryptophan possessed excellent dispersibility with the spherical model. The size distribution of 2.3–3.1 nm was observed and the average diameter was located at 2.7 ± 0.034 nm (Fig. 1B). The components and oxidation states of all elements were discussed through X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) measurement. The Au, C, N and O elements were obtained in the XPS full spectrum, and the corresponding peaks were located at 85.08 eV, 286.06 eV, 401.08 eV and 532.10 eV, respectively (Fig. S2) [25]. In XPS single spectrum of Au, two peaks about 84.98 eV and 88.58 eV were obtained, which were attributed to the Au 4f7/2 and Au 4f5/2 features of Au0 (Fig. 1C) [26]. In XPS single spectrum of C1s (Fig. 2 D), the C = C–C, C–N and C = O/C = N bonds about C were located at 2855.1 eV, 286.2 eV and 288.1 eV [27,28]. In XPS single spectrum of N1s, two peaks about 398.5 eV and 400.6 eV were observed, which were ascribed to the C-N-C and C-NH bonds of N (Fig. 1E) [29]. In XPS single spectrum of O1s, the O-C-O, C-OH and C = O bonds about O were located at 530.9 eV, 531.8 eV and 533.1 eV (Fig. 1F) [30]. The Fourier transform infrared (FT-IR) was further used to carry out the surface functional groups of Au NCs@tryptophan. In the FT-IR spectrum of Au NCs@tryptophan, the characteristic functional groups of L-tryptophan could be exhibited at 3418.7, 3343.2, 1624.4, 1600.2, 1500.4, 1460.8, 1357.3, 1123.7, 958.4 and 813.8 cm−1 (Fig. S3). To our knowledge, the peaks at 3418.7 and 3343.2 cm−1 should be the stretching vibration absorption peak of hydroxyl or amino groups [31]. The stretching vibration absorption peaks of C = C and C = O bond were distributed at 2109.2 and 1624.5 cm−1 [32]. The peaks at 1600.2, 1500.4 and 1460.8 cm−1 were ascribed to skeleton vibration of benzene ring. The peaks at 1123.7 cm−1 was attributed to the stretching vibration absorption peak of C-O bond [33]. The peaks at 1357.3 and 813.8 cm−1 were attributed to the deformation vibration of C–H and N–H bonds, respectively. The peak at 958.4 cm−1 was ascribed to the deformation vibration of OH…O = of acid. The characteristic absorption peaks of tryptophan have been successfully introduced into the Au NCs@tryptophan. Meanwhile, the FT-IR spectrum of Au NCs@tryptophan and furaltadone were compared. There was a significant difference between the FT-IR spectrum of Au NCs@tryptophan and furaltadone.

As exhibited in Fig. S4, the feature absorption peak at 375 nm should be attributed to the UV absorption peak of Au NCs@tryptophan. However, similar absorption peaks were not found in the UV absorption peak of L-tryptophan and tetrachloroauric acid, indicating the existence of the interaction between gold atom and L-tryptophan. As is known to all, the emission wavelength was obtained under the action of excitation wavelength. Then, it was necessary to study the effect of excitation wavelength on emission wavelength. As displayed in Fig. 2A, the emission wavelength was not fixed when the excitation wavelength ranged from 340 nm to 390 nm (interval 10 nm). Under the excitation wavelength at 369 nm, the emission spectra of Au NCs@tryptophan displayed a wide peak at 452 nm (Fig. 2B). When the furaltadone was added into the Au NCs@tryptophan, the fluorescence intensity reduced obviously. Meanwhile, the positions of excitation and emission peaks have not shifted. Furthermore, the emission spectra of Au NCs@tryptophan, L-tryptophan, tetrachloroauric acid and furaltadone were displayed in Fig. S5. The fluorescence originated from Au NCs@tryptophan.

The essence of Au NCs@tryptophan displayed that gold atom was regularly surrounded by L-tryptophan. The above-mentioned experimental patterns ensured the stability and water solubility of Au NCs@tryptophan. To our knowledge, the stability and water solubility of fluorescence probe were the most basic requirements for widespread application. Then, the stability of Au NCs@tryptophan was performed under different storage time, UV irradiation time and NaCl concentration. As displayed in Fig. S6A, the fluorescence intensity of Au NCs@tryptophan could maintain for two months without any weakening at low temperature (4 ℃) and room temperature. Moreover, the similar experimental results were also observed in UV irradiation and ion effects. In Fig. S6B, the strong light stabilization effect was discovered after continuous irradiation for 60 min at 365 nm. The Au NCs@tryptophan could stand the ion effect in NaCl solution for a long time, indicating that Au NCs@tryptophan had strong resistance to salt interference (Fig. S6C).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Development and mechanism of Au NCs@tryptophan based fluorescence sensor
ce:section.ce:para: The sensitivity of furaltadone determination was carried out via adjusting the pH value of buffer solution and the reaction time. As exhibited in Fig. S7A, the fluorescence responses were performed with the pH changing from 3.0 to 6.0. The F0-F reached maximum value when the pH value of buffer solution was 6.0. The fluorescence intensity of Au NCs@tryptophan with and without furaltadone were measured under various incubation time (from 30 to 180 s). It is suggested in Fig. S7B that the F0-F quickly reached its maximum value within 30 s. Thus, the pH value and reaction time were 6.0 and 30 s. In the Au NCs@tryptophan based system (Au NCs@tryptophan + buffer solution) in the presence of furaltadone at different amounts, a phenomenon of fluorescence intensity regular decrease was observed in FL emission spectra at ∼ 452 nm (Fig. 3 A). The regular and significant fluorescence weakening changes of Au NCs to furaltadone exhibited that Au NCs@tryptophan could be established as a novel fluorescence nanosensor of furaltadone. A significant linear relationship between the logarithm of relative fluorescence intensity (ln(F0/F), F0 and F represent the fluorescence intensity at 452 nm without and with the addition of furaltadone) and the furaltadone amounts was developed within the concentration range of 0.5–100 μM, the corresponding equation for linearly fitting curve was ln(F0/F) = 0.0213[Q] + 0.0186 (Fig. 3B). The detection limit was 0.087 μM, obtained from “3σ/k” (“σ” stands for the standard deviation of blank solution and “k” represents the slope of linear fitting curve) [34].

As exhibited in Fig. 4 A (furaltadone), a significant absorption peak at 362 nm was observed and any characteristic absorption peaks were not found over a larger wavelength range (>450 nm). When the excitation wavelength was set as 369 nm, the Au NCs@tryptophan displayed a wide emission peak with the range of 395–600 nm (452 nm). Specially, an overlapping feature between the excitation spectrum of Au NCs@tryptophan and the UV absorption spectrum of furaltadone. Then, the fluorescence weakening of Au NCs@tryptophan in the presence of furaltadone might be ascribed to the inner filter effect [35,36]. Moreover, the fluorescence lifetime was continuously used to carry out our inference. Based on the fluorescence lifetime data and formula (τ = (B1*t1 2 + B2*t2 2)/(B1*t1 + B2*t2)) [37], the fluorescence lifetime of Au NCs@tryptophan without and with the addition of furaltadone were 12.38 ns and 12.19 ns, respectively (Fig. 4B). The difference of fluorescence lifetime could be almost ignored. So the inner filter effect should be the main quenching mechanism [38,39].

Compared with existing literature reports for furaltadone sensing, the linear determination range and LOD of the Au NCs@tryptophan based fluorescence sensor of furaltadone were equivalent and even superior to the existing literature reports (Table 1 ). Generally, the conventional analytical methods demanded high cost equipment, cumbersome operation steps, professional personnel and long sample pre-treatment. These unfavorable factors may limit the above strategies for actual applications. On the contrary, the fluorescence detection strategies were facile, efficient, sensitive and low-priced, and could determine the amount of targets to a very low concentration level. Based on excellent performances of Au NCs@tryptophan with blue-emitting fluorescence, the developed fluorescence sensor of furaltadone could carry out the fluorescence determination of furaltadone in real samples and the naked-eye visual fluorescence imaging sensing of furaltadone.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Selectivity of Au NCs@tryptophan based fluorescence sensor
ce:section.ce:para: To carry out the selective fluorescence responses of the fluorescence sensor on furaltadone, biomolecules and ions from real human serum samples were used as the possible control substances of furaltadone, such as L-glycine, L-alanine, L-histidine, L-serine, L-proline, L-cysteine, ascorbic acid, glutathione, urea, Na+, K+, F−, Cl−, Br−, I−, CO3 2−, HCO3 − and SO4 2−. The corresponding experimental data were displayed in Fig. 5 . Apparently, the dramatic change of the relative fluorescence intensity (F0/F) after the addition of furaltadone (100 μM) was observed, and the very slight change (approaching 1) of F0/F with possible control substances (1000 μM) were obtained. In interference experiments, when the amount of possible control substances reached 10-fold higher than furaltadone, the F0/F value would not change too much either. Therefore, the developed nanosensor possessed high selectivity towards furaltadone in the presence of possible control substances. In the following experiments, the application possibilities of the developed sensor would be carried out in real samples.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fluorescence determination and visual imaging of furaltadone
ce:section.ce:para: To further assess the practicability of the as-prepared nanosensor for furaltadone determination, its application possibility was performed in lake water samples and glucose samples. The real samples with the addition of furaltadone were introduced into the Au NCs@tryptophan based fluorescence system. Based on the linear equation ln(F0/F) = 0.0213[Q] + 0.0186 in Fig. xx, the amounts of furaltadone in real samples were calculated through obtaining relevant fluorescence intensity data. As displayed in Table 2 , the obtained values of furaltadone concentration from the as-prepared sensor were equivalent to the spiked concentration. The relevant experimental results could confirm that Au NCs@tryptophan for furaltadone detection had high application value, such as recovery, relative standard deviation (RSD) and RSD distribution.

To carry out the visual fluorescence imaging sensing of furaltadone, the aqueous of Au NCs@tryptophan based detection system with different furaltadone concentration were placed under daylight lamp and ultraviolet lamp at the excitation wavelength of 365 nm. As exhibited in Fig. 6 , with increasing furaltadone concentration, the color of solution did not change under daylight lamp and the blue fluorescence gradually quenched under ultraviolet lamp. Each fluorescence color corresponded to the corresponding furaltadone concentration in the Au NCs@tryptophan based detection aqueous. Fluorescence color variety indicated the various amounts of furaltadone. The results showed the possibility of Au NCs@tryptophan for visual fluorescence imaging sensing of furaltadone.

In the present study, Au NCs with blue-emitting fluorescence were simply prepared in aqueous phase by utilizing tryptophan as the stabilizer and reducing agent. The as-developed Au NCs were stabilized by tryptophan to ensure Au NCs@tryptophan with excellent ultraviolet and salt resistance stability. When the excitation wavelength was set as 369 nm, Au NCs@tryptophan displayed a wide fluorescence emission peak around 452 nm. The presence of furaltadone led to fluorescence quenching of Au NCs@tryptophan via the inner filter effect. An excellent linear law between the logarithm of relative fluorescence intensity (ln(F0/F) and the furaltadone amounts (from 0.5 to 100 μM) was developed. The fluorescence weakening responses of Au NCs@tryptophan were excellently selective for furaltadone over possible control substances. The Au NCs@tryptophan based fluorescence sensor of furaltadone possessed an ascendant feasibility of furaltadone sensing in real samples. Dramatically, the as-prepared nano-sensor of furaltadone suggested the naked-eye visual sensing of furaltadone.
Zhifeng Cai: Conceptualization, Methodology, Investigation, Supervision, Writing – review & editing. Huinan Li: . Xin Yang: . Ming Zhang: . Jinhao Guo: . Yani Su: . Taotao Liu: .

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: We would like to thank the Shanxi Province Science Foundation for Youths (No. 20210302124210) and the College Students’ Innovation Program of Taiyuan Normal University (No. CXCY2221) for financial support.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.saa.2023.123748.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
The following are the Supplementary data to this article:
Supplementary data 1
